title: Sentences you already own
pedagogy: Sentence recombination over units 1–5 vocabulary: the ขอ + thing + หน่อย + ครับ/ค่ะ request frame (food-03) recombined with new slot fillers. Cloze drills isolate one decision per sentence — a content slot (what you're asking for) and the particle slot (ครับ male / ค่ะ female statement / คะ question, greet-02). Sentence listening contrasts the near-openers กิน (mid, mid ก + live น) / ขอ (rising, high ข + live) / เอา (mid) that differ mainly by tone and vowel.

exercises.title: You already own these sentences
body: Every sentence in this unit is built from words you've already learned — nothing new to memorize, only new combinations.
The request frame from Ordering Food — ขอ + thing + หน่อย + ครับ/ค่ะ — takes anything in its middle slot. Swap น้ำ for ข้าว and you've ordered dinner instead of water.
From here on you'll fill gaps in sentences and catch whole sentences by ear. One decision at a time.

exercises.meaning: Could I get some water, please? (male speaker)
exercises.explanation: The thing you want sits right after ขอ. ข้าว would be a fine sentence too — but it orders rice, not water. กิน is a verb and can't fill the thing-slot.
กิน (mid tone) and ขอ (rising) open almost alike — the tone is the difference between eating and asking.

exercises.meaning: Could I get some water, please? (female speaker)
exercises.explanation: A female speaker making a request ends on ค่ะ (falling). ครับ is the male particle, and high-tone คะ belongs on questions — this is a request, not a question.

exercises.meaning: Could I get some rice, please? (male speaker)
exercises.gloss: ask-for + rice + softener + male polite particle
exercises.explanation: Same frame, new filling: ขอ + ข้าว + หน่อย + ครับ. The middle slot is yours — everything you ever order goes there.
Three openers, one ear test: เอา starts on a vowel, ขอ rises, กิน stays mid. All three end in น้ำ — the first syllable decides everything.
